On the morning of June 4th, 2023, at approximately 7:50 AM, I was driving past the Riverside Chemical facility on Highway 27 when I saw a thick brown plume of smoke and what looked like a chemical mist drifting from a leaking storage tank in the south yard. The tank was clearly labeled with a hazardous-materials placard. I pulled over at the gas station across the road and watched for about ten minutes. I could see at least one worker in a hard hat trying to close a valve with no respirator on. The wind was carrying the plume east toward the residential neighborhood on Elm Street. There were no warning sirens going off and no police or fire trucks on scene at that point. I called the EPA tip line and the local fire department from the gas station. By the time the fire department arrived around 8:15 AM the plume had visibly drifted at least three blocks downwind.
